verses.text: He that loves instruction loves knowledge, but he that hates reproof is brutish; a good man obtains favour from the LORD, and 'a man shall not be established by wickedness.' Teachableness marks the wise. God roots the righteous but uproots the wicked.

He that loves instruction loves knowledge, but he that hates reproof is brutish; a good man obtains favour from the LORD, and 'a man shall not be established by wickedness.' Teachableness marks the wise. God roots the righteous but uproots the wicked.

He that loves instruction loves knowledge, but he that hates reproof is brutish; a good man obtains favour from the LORD, and 'a man shall not be established by wickedness.' Teachableness marks the wise. God roots the righteous but uproots the wicked.

A virtuous woman is a crown to her husband, but a shameful one is rottenness in his bones; the thoughts of the righteous are right, the words of the wicked a lying ambush. Character shows in home and speech. The just speak truth that delivers, not deceit that destroys.

A virtuous woman is a crown to her husband, but a shameful one is rottenness in his bones; the thoughts of the righteous are right, the words of the wicked a lying ambush. Character shows in home and speech. The just speak truth that delivers, not deceit that destroys.

A virtuous woman is a crown to her husband, but a shameful one is rottenness in his bones; the thoughts of the righteous are right, the words of the wicked a lying ambush. Character shows in home and speech. The just speak truth that delivers, not deceit that destroys.

The wicked are overthrown and gone, but the house of the righteous shall stand; a man is commended by his wisdom, and the lowly servant is better than the proud man who lacks bread. Substance beats show. God establishes the righteous and honours quiet worth over empty pride.

The wicked are overthrown and gone, but the house of the righteous shall stand; a man is commended by his wisdom, and the lowly servant is better than the proud man who lacks bread. Substance beats show. God establishes the righteous and honours quiet worth over empty pride.

The wicked are overthrown and gone, but the house of the righteous shall stand; a man is commended by his wisdom, and the lowly servant is better than the proud man who lacks bread. Substance beats show. God establishes the righteous and honours quiet worth over empty pride.

A righteous man regards the life of his beast, but the wicked's mercies are cruel; he that tills his land shall be satisfied, while the idle chase vanity. Kindness and diligence go together. The godly are tender even to their animals and faithful in honest work.

A righteous man regards the life of his beast, but the wicked's mercies are cruel; he that tills his land shall be satisfied, while the idle chase vanity. Kindness and diligence go together. The godly are tender even to their animals and faithful in honest work.

A righteous man regards the life of his beast, but the wicked's mercies are cruel; he that tills his land shall be satisfied, while the idle chase vanity. Kindness and diligence go together. The godly are tender even to their animals and faithful in honest work.

The wicked is snared by his own lips, but the just escapes trouble; a man is satisfied by the fruit of his mouth, and 'the way of a fool is right in his own eyes.' Words reap their harvest. The wise heed counsel, while fools trust their own judgment.

The wicked is snared by his own lips, but the just escapes trouble; a man is satisfied by the fruit of his mouth, and 'the way of a fool is right in his own eyes.' Words reap their harvest. The wise heed counsel, while fools trust their own judgment.

A fool's wrath is known at once, but the prudent covers shame; truth endures, lying is momentary; rash words pierce like a sword, but 'the tongue of the wise is health.' Speech wounds or heals. The wise restrain anger and speak words that mend.

The lip of truth is established for ever, but a lying tongue for a moment; deceit fills the hearts of mischief-makers, but counsellors of peace have joy; no real evil befalls the just. Truth lasts; deceit is fleeting. God guards the righteous from lasting harm.

'Lying lips are abomination to the LORD,' but those who deal truly are His delight; the prudent conceal knowledge, and the diligent hand shall bear rule, while the slothful is put to forced labour. Honesty and diligence are honoured. God loves truth and lifts the industrious.

'Lying lips are abomination to the LORD,' but those who deal truly are His delight; the prudent conceal knowledge, and the diligent hand shall bear rule, while the slothful is put to forced labour. Honesty and diligence are honoured. God loves truth and lifts the industrious.

Heaviness in the heart makes it stoop, but a good word makes it glad; the righteous guide their neighbour, while the wicked seduce; the slothful roasts not what he caught, but 'in the way of righteousness is life.' Kind words and faithful work bless. The path of righteousness leads to life, not death.
